source: Collaborative Knowledge Base Embedding for Recommender Systems

concepts.finding: CKE: collaborative knowledge base embedding for recommendation. Combines three embedding models: (1) TransR for structural knowledge (entity-relation triples), (2) stacked autoencoder for textual content, (3) stacked denoising autoencoder for visual content. All embeddings jointly optimized with collaborative filtering.
concepts.implication: Multi-modal keyword embedding: structural (taxonomy hierarchy, keyword-intent relationships) + textual (keyword text, description) + contextual (co-occurrence). All modalities contribute to intent representation.
concepts.open_question: Which modality (structural, textual, contextual) contributes most to keyword intent accuracy? How to weight different modalities?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A multi-modal CKE-style keyword embedding (combining taxonomy structure with text and co-occurrence context) outperforms any single-modality embedding for intent clustering.
